text: Replace hardcoded frontend backend/broker endpoints with runtime configuration
description: Remove hardcoded `localhost` websocket and API targets from the frontend. The built UI should connect to the backend and broker using resolved runtime configuration or same-origin-compatible wiring so build mode works reliably and LAN/browser access is not misleading.

reflections.context: Remove hardcoded localhost frontend endpoint assumptions that were breaking build-mode local runs and misleading LAN/browser access.
reflections.outcome: Frontend websocket and watchdog session URLs now resolve host and protocol from the browser at runtime while keeping configured backend and broker ports. The frontend type-check and production build both passed after the change.
reflections.effectiveness: High for a small patch. Centralizing URL construction fixed three separate connection sites without changing backend protocol behavior.
reflections.lesson: For browser builds, hostname and protocol should come from the browser environment, not from server-side assumptions baked into source strings.
reflections.pattern: Similar to other local-first tooling issues where the runtime topology is correct but the browser client accidentally assumes it is always running on the same machine as the backend.

status.comment: Implemented browser-safe runtime endpoint resolution in the frontend. tRPC backend and broker clients now derive host/protocol from the browser instead of hardcoded localhost, and spawned watchdog sockets connect back to the active browser host. Verified with `npm run type-check -w frontend` and `npm run build-only -w frontend`.
